hero.subtitle: From the Primordial Waters of Creation
hero.title: The Infinite Cycle
hero.lore: Before time, before earth, before the sky was woven — there was only the endless dark water. From its depths rose Ak Ana, the White Mother, carrying the breath of creation — Kut. She whispered, and the cosmos stirred into being.

migration.heading: The Path of
migration.desc: Follow the Celestial Grey Wolf across the iron mountains and endless steppes — from imprisonment to liberation, from winter's grip to spring's rebirth.

ergenekon.title: Ergenekon
ergenekon.subtitle: The Iron Mountain Prison
ergenekon.text: After a devastating defeat, the surviving Turkic tribes retreated into a hidden valley enclosed by impassable iron mountains. For generations they forged their identity in the fires of patience.

goc.title: The Great Migration
goc.subtitle: Birth of Empires
goc.text: When a blacksmith melted the iron mountain with a great forge, the Turkic people poured forth like a river of steel, reborn and unstoppable. Guided by Gökbörü, the Celestial Wolf.

steppes.title: The Endless Steppes
steppes.subtitle: Home of the Free
steppes.text: Across the vast grasslands of Central Asia they spread — horse-riders, sky-worshippers, keepers of the wolf banner. Each tribe carrying the sacred fire of Tengri.

worldTree.heading: Bayterek
worldTree.p1: At the center of all existence stands Bayterek — the sacred World Tree whose roots descend into the Abyss of Tamag, whose trunk sustains the mortal realm of the Steppes, and whose branches reach into the celestial palace of Tengri.
worldTree.p2: The World Tree is the axis of all cosmology — a bridge between gods and mortals, between life and death. Every soul, every spirit, every saga flows through its veins.

ritual.heading: The Ritual
ritual.desc: The shamanic drum is the bridge between worlds. Each symbol opens a portal to the spirits that guard nature, water, and the forgotten realms.
ritual.drumDesc: Click the symbols to reveal the hidden guardians

sun.name: Kün — The Sun
sun.lore: The eye of Tengri, the Eternal Blue Sky. The sun watches over the Upper World and bestows Kut — divine life force — upon the worthy. Shamans journey toward the sun during their celestial ascent.

moon.name: Ay — The Moon
moon.lore: Guardian of the night and keeper of dreams. The moon governs the tides of the spirit world. Under its light, the veil between worlds grows thin and shamans can commune with the dead.

tree.name: Bayterek — World Tree
tree.lore: The axis of all cosmology. Through the drum, shamans climb the World Tree — ascending its seventeen branches to reach the realm of Ülgen, or descending its roots to negotiate with Erlik Han.

wolf.name: Börü — The Wolf
wolf.lore: Sacred guide and ancestor spirit. The wolf on the drum represents Asena, the divine she-wolf who birthed the Turkic people. Shamans call upon her spirit for protection and guidance.

eagle.name: Kartal — The Eagle
eagle.lore: Messenger between worlds. The eagle carries prayers from the Middle World to Tengri's throne. The first shaman was said to have been raised by an eagle in the crown of Bayterek.

water.name: Su — The Water
water.lore: The primordial element from which all creation emerged. Su İyesi, the Water Spirit, dwells in every river, lake, and spring. Offerings of milk and bread ensure her blessing.

suIyesi.name: Su İyesi — Water Guardian
suIyesi.desc: She dwells in every river, lake, and spring. Beautiful and terrifying, she can grant fertile harvests or drag the unworthy into the depths. Fishermen whisper prayers before casting their nets.
suIyesi.offering: Offerings: Milk, bread, and silver coins at the water's edge

ormanIyesi.name: Orman İyesi — Forest Guardian
ormanIyesi.desc: Master of every tree, root, and creature of the wild. He appears as a towering figure of bark and moss, or a whispering wind through the canopy. Those who respect the forest receive his protection.
ormanIyesi.warning: Warning: Never cut a tree without asking the Forest Spirit's permission

sun.title: Gün Ana — Sun Mother
sun.desc: The life-giving solar deity, revered as the source of warmth and light. She dwells in the highest celestial layer.

moon.title: Ay Ata — Moon Father
moon.desc: The ancestral guardian of the night, guiding nomadic paths across the endless steppes and ruling the tides.
moon.offering: Warning: Never point at the full moon.

eagle.title: Karakuş — Divine Eagle
eagle.desc: The messenger of the heavens. It carries prayers to Kayra Han and watches over the sacred mountains.

tree.title: Bayterek — World Tree
tree.desc: The cosmic axis connecting the underworld, earth, and heavens. It channels energy across dimensions.

wolf.title: Kökbörü — Sky Wolf
wolf.desc: The divine guide of the Turkic peoples, leading them out of Ergenekon. A symbol of resilience and freedom.
wolf.offering: The eternal spirit of the pack.

water.title: Su İyesi — Water Guardian
water.desc: She dwells in every river, lake, and spring. Beautiful and terrifying, she can grant fertile harvests or drag the unworthy into the depths.
water.offering: Offerings: Silver coins at the waters edge.

forest.title: Orman İyesi — Forest Guardian
forest.desc: Master of every tree, root, and creature of the wild. He appears as a whispering wind through the canopy.
forest.offering: Warning: Never cut a tree without asking permission.

pantheon.heading: The Divine
pantheon.desc: The gods who shaped the cosmos and govern the fate of every soul beneath the Eternal Blue Sky.

kayra.name: Kayra Han
kayra.desc: The supreme creator who existed before all. Father of Ülgen and Erlik, he shaped the two brothers and set them upon their eternal paths — one to light, one to darkness.

ulgen.name: Ülgen
ulgen.desc: Ruler of the Upper World with seven sons who embody the elements of nature. Ülgen guides shamans through the sky's seventeen layers and bestows the sacred Kut upon the worthy.

kyzagan.name: Kyzagan
kyzagan.desc: Son of Ülgen and guardian of warriors. Kyzagan rides the crimson storm, his blazing sword cleaving the sky. He blesses armies with courage and judges the valor of fallen heroes.

pantheon.nexusDesc: Hover over a deity to reveal their connections and influence over the realms

bestiary.heading: Bestiary of the
bestiary.desc: Creatures and spirits that roam between the realms — companions of heroes, products of curses, and guardians of nature.

itbarak.name: Itbarak
itbarak.desc: A legendary race of fierce warriors with human bodies and dog heads, mentioned in the Epic of Oghuz Khagan.
itbarak.lore1: They were so savage and powerful that they defeated the great Oghuz Khagan's army in their first encounter.
itbarak.lore2: Later, Oghuz Khagan gathered his strength, returned, and vanquished this dark tribe, making them a terrifying steppe legend.

bukre.name: Bukre
bukre.desc: Divine steed of legendary heroes, Bukre soars between the clouds on feathered wings of pure light.
bukre.lore1: Bukre is a dragon-like creature in Turkic mythology, often depicted as a winged horse or a powerful steed with magical abilities. It is said to be the companion of heroes and a symbol of strength and courage.
bukre.lore2: In the sagas of Manas and Oghuz, the Bukre carries its rider through impossible battles and across the boundaries between the living and the dead.
bukre.connection: Companion of Heroes · Cursed by Erlik Khan

tulpar.name: Tulpar
tulpar.desc: Divine steed of legendary heroes, Tulpar soars between the clouds on feathered wings of pure light.
tulpar.lore1: Born from the tears of Ülgen when he wept for banished heroes, the Tulpar is the most sacred companion a warrior can earn. Only those deemed worthy by the Sky God may ride upon its back through the seventeen layers of heaven.
tulpar.lore2: In the sagas of Manas and Oghuz, the Tulpar carries its rider through impossible battles and across the boundaries between the living and the dead.
tulpar.connection: Companion of Heroes · Blessed by Ülgen

tepegoz.name: Tepegöz
tepegoz.desc: A terrifying cyclops born of a curse, terrorizing the Oghuz tribes until Basat the hero defeats it.
tepegoz.lore1: Tepegöz was born from an unnatural union between a shepherd and a Peri (fairy). The creature grew into an unstoppable giant with a single eye, devouring sixty men per day.
tepegoz.lore2: Only the hero Basat — raised by a lion and blessed with divine courage — could face the beast and pierce its single eye with a red-hot iron stake.
tepegoz.connection: Product of a Curse · Slain by Basat

arcura.name: Arçura
arcura.desc: Guardian of the wild, Arçura lures wanderers into the deep forest and can grant wisdom — or madness.
arcura.lore1: Arçura dwells in the deepest, most ancient forests of the Turkic lands. She appears in many forms — sometimes as a beautiful maiden, sometimes as a towering figure covered in bark and moss.
arcura.lore2: Shamans would commune with Arçura to gain knowledge of medicinal herbs and the spirits of nature.

asena.name: Asena
asena.desc: Sacred mother wolf of the Turkic peoples, she nursed the last surviving boy and gave birth to ten tribes.
asena.lore1: When enemies slaughtered the entire Turkic tribe, a single wounded boy was left for dead. A celestial she-wolf, Asena, found the child and nursed him back to health in a hidden valley surrounded by mountains.
asena.lore2: Asena is the most sacred symbol of Turkic identity — the divine wolf who ensured the survival and rebirth of an entire civilization. The wolf banner flies at the front of every great army.
asena.connection: Mother of Tribes · Blessed by Tengri

alkarisi.title: Alkarisi
alkarisi.desc: One of Erlik Khan's oldest shadows on earth. Often depicted in red garments, targeting fresh life energy and carrying souls to the lord of the underworld.
alkarisi.weak: Iron, the color red, and Syrian rue.

shadow.heading: SHADOW
shadow.desc: Ancient depths where the first seeds of darkness were sown, ruled by silence and judgment.
shadow.erlikP1: Absolute ruler of the underworld in Turkic mythology. The first being exiled from the heavens and the lord of death.
shadow.erlikP2: Guardian of darkness and balance, sitting beneath the nine-branched black pine with his silver staff.
shadow.alkarisiDesc: A malevolent spirit that haunts postpartum women, stealing their life energy (Kut).
shadow.alkarisiLore2: Drawn to the vulnerable, particularly during childbirth.

bukre.name: Bukre
bukre.desc: A wise, wingless dragon representing goodness and balance, known for battling the evil dragon Sangal.
bukre.title: Legend of Bukre
bukre.lore2: A guardian of the underworld's deepest secrets, snaking through Tamag.

kulliyat.title: DARK
kulliyat.subtitle: The secret history of malevolent spirits and ancient creatures shaped by the will of Erlik Khan.
kulliyat.footer: This information is compiled from ancient Turkic mythological sources.

alkarisi.title: Alkarisi
alkarisi.desc: One of Erlik Khan's oldest shadows on earth. Often depicted in red garments, targeting fresh life energy and carrying souls to the lord of the underworld.
alkarisi.weak: Iron, the color red, and Syrian rue.

sagas.heading: The Epic
sagas.desc: Tales woven into the very fabric of the steppes — of iron mountains, divine wolves, and the unbreakable spirit of a people.

ergenekon.title: The Saga of Ergenekon
ergenekon.p1: After a devastating defeat, the surviving Turkic tribes retreated into Ergenekon — a hidden valley enclosed by impassable iron mountains. For generations they lived in isolation, growing strong and numerous, forging their identity in the fires of patience.
ergenekon.p2: When the valley could no longer hold them, a blacksmith melted the iron mountain itself with a great forge. The Turkic people poured forth like a river of steel, reborn and unstoppable. The day of their emergence is celebrated as Nevruz — the New Day.

oghuz.title: The Epic of Oghuz Khagan
oghuz.p1: Oghuz Khagan was born with the face of the sky, the strength of the mountain, and the heart of the wolf. From infancy, he spoke to eagles and commanded rivers. He conquered the four corners of the world, guided by a divine grey wolf.
oghuz.p2: He divided his empire among his six sons — three of the Sun (Bozok) and three of the Moon (Üçok) — creating the foundation of all great Turkic dynasties that would shape the world for millennia.

asena.title: The Legend of Asena & The Great Migration
asena.p1: When enemies destroyed the Turkic people, only a single boy survived, grievously wounded. A celestial she-wolf, Asena, found the dying child and nursed him in a secret cave among the Altai Mountains.
asena.p2: From their divine bond came ten sons — each the father of a great Turkic tribe. When the population outgrew the sacred valley, they migrated across the vast steppes of Central Asia, carrying the wolf banner as their eternal symbol.

footer.title: The Infinite Cycle
footer.subtitle: A Digital Museum of Turkic Cosmology
footer.copyright: © The Infinite Cycle · Inspired by Ancient Turkic Mythology

influenceMap.title: The Web of Creation
influenceMap.desc: Every spirit, creature, and legend flows from a single source — Kayra Han, the Primordial Creator
influenceMap.hover: Hover to reveal the spirit threads

ergenekon.p1: After a devastating defeat, only two persons from the Turkic tribes, Kiyan and Nukuz, and their wives remained. While fleeing from the enemies, they found a passage between steep mountains so narrow that only a deer could pass.
ergenekon.p2: When they passed through this passage, they found a secret and paradise-like valley surrounded by impassable high mountains, where rivers, fruit trees, and game animals were abundant. They named this place Ergenekon, meaning 'mine place'.
ergenekon.p3: They lived in Ergenekon for four hundred years and multiplied so much that they could no longer fit in this valley. They could not find the narrow passage their ancestors used to come here. While exploring the surroundings of the mountains, they noticed that there was iron ore in only one place.
ergenekon.p4: A blacksmith said that this mountain could be melted. They lined up a row of wood and a row of coal around the mountain. They made huge bellows from seventy hides. When they pulled the bellows together, the fire became so fierce that the iron mountain melted and a road large enough for a loaded camel to pass was opened.
ergenekon.p5: That day became the day of rebirth for the Turks. They emerged from Ergenekon under the guidance of a celestial Grey Wolf (Gökböri) and dominated all the steppes again by taking revenge on their ancestors.

oghuz.title: THE EPIC OF OGHUZ KHAGAN
oghuz.subtitle: The Sky-Faced Khagan
oghuz.p1: Oghuz Khagan was born as a child with a face blue like the sky, a mouth red like fire, and eyes bright like diamonds. He started to speak, walk, and eat raw meat when he was only forty days old. As the shadow of Tengri on earth, he was a master of using all kinds of weapons.
oghuz.p2: One day, he killed a huge monster (Kiyant) that threatened his people by himself. After this victory, he married a divine woman who appeared in a beam of light. He had three sons from her named Sun, Moon, and Star. Later, he married another woman he found in a tree hollow and had three sons from her named Sky, Mountain, and Sea.
oghuz.p3: One day, while Oghuz Khagan was on a campaign with his army, a large male wolf with a celestial mane appeared before him. The wolf said, 'O Oghuz, wherever you go, I will walk before you.' The Turkic army followed this divine guide and conquered the four corners of the world.
oghuz.p4: When Oghuz Khagan grew old, he distributed his golden bow and three silver arrows among his sons. He divided his country into Bozoks and Uchoks. He advised his children to stay in unity and solidarity. His legacy became the foundation of the great Turkic dynasties that would rule for millennia.

asena.title: THE LEGEND OF ASENA & THE GREAT MIGRATION
asena.subtitle: The Sacred Wolf of Rebirth
asena.p1: In ancient times, the Turkic tribes were subjected to a large enemy attack. All the people were slaughtered, only a small child with his arms and legs cut off was left behind. The enemies left him in a swamp, thinking he would die.
asena.p2: Asena, a she-wolf descended from the sky, found the child and healed his wounds by licking them. She ensured his growth by feeding him with meat every day. When the child grew up, he became the mate of the wolf. When the enemies found out that the child was alive and came to kill him, Asena flew and took refuge in a hidden cave among the Altai Mountains.
asena.p3: Behind the cave was a paradise-like valley. Asena gave birth to ten boys there. These boys grew up and married girls from outside, forming the ten great Turkic tribes. Their lineage multiplied so much that they could no longer fit in the valley.
asena.p4: Under the guidance of the Divine Wolf, they decided to leave the valley and migrate to the open steppes. Carrying the wolf banner at the front, they returned to the lands of their ancestors. This great migration was the beginning of the eternal dominance of the Turks in the steppes.
